name: N.y.cima Roll
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------CIMA MIX-------------------------
1/2 c Finely chopped onions
2 tbsp Olive oil
1 c Whole wheat bread crumbs
1/4 lb Ground seitan
1/4 lb Fine soy grits
1/4 lb Hand crushed firm tofu
1/3 c Nutritional yeast
2 tsp Dried marjoram
2 tsp Dried thyme
2 tsp Salt
1 1/2 c Water
1 c Chopped spinach, packed
1/4 c Chopped roasted red pepper
1/2 c Pistachios
1 c Green peas
1 tbsp Agar flakes
1/2 c Gluten flour
2 tbsp Liquid lecithin
Rinse tofu and pat dry. Cube and puree in a food processor with all ingredients except Cima mix.
Shape the Cima mix into a 12-inch by 3-inch roll. Spread the tofu puree on a 12-inch by 28-inch piece of plastic wrap and roll around the Cima log. Smooth out the seam. Steam for 30 minutes, let cool for 10 minutes, then slice and serve.
Note: You MUST have a steamer wide enough to support the entire roll from underneath! If you don't, it will slip and come unrolled. It'll still get cooked, but rather than a pretty, colorful roll to slice, you'll have a serious mess. This shouldn't be a problem, but for a broke person cooking for nine @someone else's apartment, these hardware problems have a nasty way of only becoming apparent an hour before mealtimes. (sigh)
CIMA MIX: (makes 4 1/2 C)
